import React from 'react'; import { AppView } from '../types'; interface HomeProps { onStart: () => void; } const Home: React.FC = ({ onStart }) => { return (
{/* Main Title */}
🎬

Emotion-Preserving Cross-Lingual Dubbing Studio

{/* Welcome Section */}

Welcome to the Dubbing Studio

🔗

Transform your media with AI-powered emotional dubbing. This platform provides a complete dubbing experience with:

{/* Features & Pages Grid */}
{/* Features */}

🎯 Features

  • • Multi-Language Support: Translate and dub into Tamil, Hindi, French, German, Spanish, and English
  • • Emotion Preservation: Advanced prosody and emotion detection to maintain the original voice characteristics
  • • Voice Cloning: Optional same-voice cloning for natural results
  • • Real-time Monitoring: Track processing with live metrics and plots
  • • Comprehensive Analysis: Detailed metrics on pitch, energy, emotion, and more
{/* Pages */}

🎴 Pages

Use the sidebar to navigate:

  1. 1. 🎙️ Dubbing Workspace - Upload media and create dubbed content
  2. 2. 📊 Analysis - View detailed results and metrics from your dubbing jobs
  3. 3. 📈 Real-Time Plots - Monitor live processing metrics and performance
{/* Getting Started */}

🚀 Getting Started

  1. 1. Go to the Dubbing Workspace page
  2. 2. Upload an audio or video file
  3. 3. Select your target language
  4. 4. Click "Run Dubbing" and wait for processing
  5. 5. Check the Analysis page for detailed results
  6. 6. Monitor processing with Real-Time Plots
{/* Bottom Info Grid */}
{/* System Info */}

📊 System Information

  • • Framework: Streamlit (Web UI)
  • • ASR Model: Whisper (with ffmpeg fallback)
  • • Translation: NLLB-200-distilled-600M
  • • TTS: XTTS-v2 or Tacotron2-DDC (fallback)
  • • Prosody: LibROSA audio analysis
  • • Emotion: Speech emotion recognition
{/* Supported Languages */}

✨ Supported Languages

  • • 🇮🇳 Tamil (ta)

This will involve storing the `latestJobResults`, `metadata`, and other relevant data directly in `App.tsx`, and passing it down as props. I am aiming to update `Dashboard.tsx` to handle a callback to save new results and update `Analysis.tsx` and `DubbingReport.tsx` to consume the new shared state. I believe this will ensure the real-time plots update properly.

To make the **Real-Time Plot** and **Analysis** pages dynamic, we need to "Lift State Up" to the main `App.tsx`. Currently, your dubbing results are trapped inside the `Dashboard` component's local state, which is why other pages can't see them.

I will move the dubbing result storage to the `App` component and pass it down as props to all views.

### Specification of Updates:
1.  **Global Result Tracking**: `App.tsx` will now maintain a `latestResults` state. When a dubbing job finishes in the Workspace, it updates this global state.
2.  **Dynamic Analysis View**: `Analysis.tsx` will now read from the global state. If a job exists, it will display the actual "Emotion Confidence," "Detected Emotion," and "Quality Score" instead of static "0.0" values.
3.  **Dynamic Dubbing Report**: `DubbingReport.tsx` will populate the "Job Overview," "Transcription," and "Prosody" sections using the real data from the Gemini API.
4.  **Audio Persistence**: The dubbed audio URL will be shared globally, so you can play the results in the workspace and immediately compare them in the Analysis report.
